data.title: Hepsor AS Q4 2025 revenue: 4.5M euros (Q4 2024: 10.5M). Full year revenue: 35.4M euros (2024: 38.4M).
data.description: In the fourth quarter of 2025, the Group reported consolidated revenue of 4.5 million euros, a significant decrease from 10.5 million euros in the same quarter of 2024. The total consolidated revenue for the entire reporting year was 35.4 million euros, down from 38.4 million euros in 2024.

data.title: Trading of Japanese government bonds, previously slow, turned into a 'battlefield' as yields surged due to debt fears.
data.description: The trading activity of Japanese government bonds, traditionally stagnant, has experienced a resurgence as concerns regarding the nation's debt have led to a significant increase in yields.

data.title: Market boosted by Meta-Nvidia deal, with Nvidia rising and AMD, Broadcom slipping. Dow Jones shows slight gains.
data.description: On Tuesday, the stock market, which was previously divided, managed to reverse its trend and achieved minimal gains. The late surge of Nvidia was due to a deal with Meta related to artificial intelligence (AI), whereas AMD and Broadcom experienced declines. This movement in the market was highlighted in the post about Dow Jones Futures, where the Meta-Nvidia deal contributed to the rise of Nvidia and impacted its AI competitors.

data.title: Taiwan Tourism Administration showcased at Phoenix Travel & Adventure Show 2026 from Feb 14-15, attracting strong interest.
data.description: The Taiwan Tourism Administration (TTA) concluded a successful exhibition at the Phoenix Travel & Adventure Show 2026 on February 14-15 at the Phoenix Convention Center. The event, which drew significant attention, was facilitated by TTA's Los Angeles Office.

data.title: Polymarket targets China market, hires Mandarin-speaking staff, offers Lunar New Year bets, appealing to Chinese users.
data.description: Cryptocurrency prediction platform Polymarket is expanding into the Chinese market, despite strict regulations against online gambling. The company is targeting growth in China by hiring Mandarin-speaking staff and offering bets related to the Lunar New Year festivities. According to reports, Polymarket is focusing on appealing to Chinese users by introducing China-specific markets, such as those centered around the Spring Festival Gala. Justin Yang, responsible for Polymarket's strategy in Asia, highlighted the significance of China as a key geography for the company. One market example includes "Which robot dancer brands will feature at the 2026 Spring Festival Gala?" showcasing the company's effort to tailor offerings to Chinese preferences.
